articles.title: Air India to operate non-stop Delhi-Toronto flights from August 1 with new Boeing 787-9 aircraft
articles.summary: Air India will launch non-stop flights between Delhi and Toronto from August 1 by deploying its new Boeing 787-9 aircraft, eliminating the current fuel stop in Vienna.

articles.title: Stored produce in a cold storage? This ₹31.54 lakh consumer court ruling could protect your harvest
articles.summary: The commission’s ruling is a sharp reminder that cold storage operators cannot treat farmers' produce as their own, especially when there is no proof of consent for sale.

articles.title: 'We literally have the paper': US-India trade deal could be signed in 3-4 months
articles.summary: Negotiations between the two countries have largely been completed, and the remaining delay is linked to Washington's ongoing trade investigations, a senior US official told Reuters

articles.title: Space, cars and AI: How Elon Musk built the world’s most powerful empire
articles.summary: Elon Musk’s empire spans SpaceX, Tesla, AI and social media. Explore how risky bets, futuristic technology and relentless ambition built one of the world’s most powerful business empires.

articles.title: Charging to 100% every night is actually bad for your EV battery. Here's why
articles.summary: Never charge to 100% daily, avoid frequent fast charging and protect from extreme temperatures. Here are expert-backed tips to keep your EV battery healthy and efficient for years.

articles.title: Any takers? Jeet Adani says airline business doesn’t make the cut
articles.summary: The government is weighing a proposal to allow airport operators to own and run airlines. But will there be any takers, especially after the Adani Group said the sector does not fit its capital discipline?

articles.title: 'We literally have the paper': US says India trade deal could be signed in months
articles.summary: A significant India-US trade agreement is expected within three to four months. Negotiations are nearly finalized, with the text effectively complete and ready for signing. The primary remaining hurdle involves ongoing US trade investigations, not bilateral disagreements.

articles.title: Space’s darkest chapters: The missions that ended in tragedy before reaching glory
articles.summary: From Soyuz to Challenger and Columbia, explore the darkest moments of space exploration where technical failures, human risks and tragic missions changed the future of space safety.

articles.title: Running on rollovers: Pakistan's reserves get $27 billion foreign loans from Saudi, China
articles.summary: Pakistan secured approximately twenty-seven billion dollars in foreign funding last fiscal year. Saudi Arabia and China provided significant rollover support for existing loans. Fresh borrowing accounted for sixteen billion dollars of the total inflows.

articles.title: Universal provident fund scheme: How PF for gig workers, self-employed could work & why it would be a game-changer
articles.summary: The proposed framework aims to allow unorganised sector workers and self-employed to set aside a portion of their income for deposits under a universal provident fund scheme which will earn regular interest on par with the existing EPFO scheme.

articles.title: Bajaj Auto is launching 12 models by September — here's every bike confirmed so far
articles.summary: Bajaj Auto plans 12 new models by September — including 10 facelifts and a new 150cc Pulsar — plus two new 125cc brands. KTM exports rose 20%, Triumph up 40% in the latest quarter.

articles.title: 2026 Maybach GLS 680 gets 603 HP, a new Hyperscreen and massage footrests — here's everything
articles.summary: The 2026 Mercedes-Maybach GLS 680 gets a new Hyperscreen, 603 hp V8 with 850 Nm, 15-speaker Burmester audio, improved massage seats and illuminated MAYBACH badging throughout.

articles.title: These Indian destinations feel like another planet — but they are closer than you think
articles.summary: Explore India’s surreal destinations that look like another planet — from Spiti Valley and Pangong Lake to living root bridges, volcano islands and mysterious crater lakes.

articles.title: MrBeast just got married on a private island — and the wedding was nothing like his videos
articles.summary: MrBeast married Thea Booysen at Richard Branson's Necker Island in a private ceremony for 70 guests. Here's inside the $2.6 billion YouTuber's wedding, life story and business empire.

articles.title: These islands did not exist before — humans created them from nothing
articles.summary: From Dubai’s Palm Jumeirah to the Netherlands’ Flevopolder, explore the world’s largest artificial islands built by humans using sand, rocks and ambitious engineering.

articles.title: A space mechanic is here: SpaceX launches robot that could save billions
articles.summary: SpaceX has launched a robotic satellite repair mission with 10-foot arms that could extend spacecraft life, reduce replacement costs and create a new billion-dollar space servicing industry.

articles.title: Diya Binu ousted: How India's youngest municipal chairperson lost power in dramatic Kerala vote
articles.summary: Her elevation was seen as a significant moment for youth participation in local governance, with many hailing her as a symbol of emerging young leadership in Kerala politics

articles.title: Boiling point? Brent crude touches five week high, here’s what analysts expect next for oil markets
articles.summary: Oil prices are climbing as geopolitical tensions between the US and Iran intensify. Analysts expect crude prices to remain within a specific range for now. Current market prices do not indicate a major structural disruption to global oil supplies.

articles.title: Why did the stock market crash today? BSE Sensex ends over 700 points down, Nifty50 below 24,000; top reasons for fall
articles.summary: Pharmaceutical stocks were among the biggest drags on the market after Trump announced a phased tariff framework for imported generic medicines, giving drugmakers a two-year transition period before higher duties come into force.

articles.title: No display, no distractions: Garmin launches a fitness band for the anti-smartwatch era
articles.summary: Garmin’s Cirqa Smart Band removes the smartwatch screen to offer distraction-free fitness tracking with 80+ workouts, health monitoring and a 10-day battery life.

articles.title: 10 sports, 70 nations: Inside the biggest changes coming to Commonwealth Games
articles.summary: Glasgow 2026 Commonwealth Games will see major changes with only 10 sports, 70+ nations, fewer events and a new compact format after a dramatic hosting crisis.

articles.title: ₹689 crore approved for EV charging network under PM E-DRIVE, but no funds spent yet
articles.summary: The latest figures indicate that the Centre has largely completed spending under FAME-II and has now shifted its focus to PM E-DRIVE for expanding the country's charging infrastructure.

articles.title: Goldman warns: Oil could surge past $120 if Hormuz disruptions continue
articles.summary: Global oil markets have been shaken this month as renewed fighting between the US and Iran pushed Brent back above $91 a barrel

articles.title: Donald Trump’s 200% tariffs on pharma: Why India’s generic medicines could still remain competitive
articles.summary: US President Donald Trump on Tuesday announced the final stage of his pharmaceutical tariff plan, under which imported generic medicines will continue to enter the US without duties until August 1, 2028.

articles.title: Paytm wallet to return? One97 Communications takes first step to relaunch wallet, applies for RBI PPI licence
articles.summary: One97 Communications disclosed the development in its June quarter investor presentation, saying the proposed wallet service would complement its existing payment offerings and provide consumers with an additional payment option.

articles.title: ₹64 crore cyber scam unravels after teen behind AI-driven fake banking apps arrested by Surat police
articles.summary: Shakya sold the malware to cyber fraud gangs through a subscription-based model, charging between ₹10,000 and ₹15,000 per month

articles.title: 'Discussed trade, tariffs & critical minerals': Jaishankar meets Rubio in Manila
Stuck at ports: Why several fertiliser vessels are stranded at Indian ports during peak kharif sowing season
articles.summary: Congestion has affected several major ports, including Kandla, Mundra, Jawaharlal Nehru Port (Nhava Sheva) and Chennai. Industry officials said that the situation has been aggravated by the rerouting of vessels due to geopolitical tensions.

articles.title: New worry for oil market: Tankers carrying Saudi crude for India, China make a U-turn in Red Sea on Houthi threats
articles.summary: On Monday, the Houthis announced a naval blockade on Saudi Arabia, potentially opening another front in the US-Israeli conflict with Iran and increasing risks to global energy supplies and international trade beyond the Gulf region.

articles.title: Pharma stocks bleed after Trump announces upto 200% tariff on generic drugs
articles.summary: Pharmaceutical stocks faced pressure after US President Trump unveiled a phased tariff plan for imported generic medicines. This plan introduces steep duties on generic drugs after a two-year tariff-free window.

articles.title: Rupee falls 11 paise to 96.36 against US dollar amid US-Iran tensions, rising crude
articles.summary: The Indian rupee opened lower on Wednesday, facing pressure from rising crude oil prices. Geopolitical tensions and demand for safe-haven assets also weighed on the domestic currency. The Reserve Bank of India's measures are helping to cushion the rupee's decline.

articles.title: Asian stocks trade in green: Kospi adds 6%, Nikkei jumps over 1,100 points
articles.summary: Asian markets opened higher, mirroring Wall Street's gains as technology stocks rebounded. Japan, South Korea, and Taiwan led the advance, while China also saw gains. Oil prices continued to climb due to threats in the Red Sea region.

articles.title: 0%, 100%, then 200%: How Trump's new generic drug tariff plan could hit India
articles.summary: Donald Trump announced phased tariffs on imported generic medicines starting August 2026. These tariffs will gradually increase to 200 percent over three years. This policy aims to reshore pharmaceutical production back into America.

Food products create most jobs among PLI sectors
Gold, silver rate live updates today: Global cues, Fed outlook and geopolitics set to steer gold, silver prices
Stock market today: Sensex closes 715 points lower; Nifty ends below 24,000
Karnataka sees fastest FDI rise among major states
Top stocks to buy today: Stock recommendations for July 22, 2026 - check list
articles.summary: Stock market recommendations: BSE, Reliance Industries, and PI Industries have been identified as the top stocks to buy today, July 22, 2026, by Mehul Kothari, DVP - Technical Research at Anand Rathi Shares.

articles.title: Does money buy happiness? What India's spending power reveals about how we live
articles.summary: Indian households now spend more on experiences and lifestyle choices. Average monthly spending has risen across both rural and urban areas. Food now constitutes a smaller portion of household expenditure.
